appliedUpdates.description: Documented that Google OAuth token at ~/.hermes/google_token.json expires on 2026-04-10 and requires re-authentication. Added note that positive-control condition for routing tests is verified via /health endpoint showing enabledSources includes both ATS and browser lanes.

summary: Tested 6 assertions for routing-enforcement milestone. 2 passed (VAL-ROUTE-001, VAL-ROUTE-006) demonstrating correct browser_only routing: only grounded_web executed, ATS lanes excluded with explicit zero-count skip evidence. 4 blocked (VAL-ROUTE-002, VAL-ROUTE-003, VAL-ROUTE-004, VAL-ROUTE-005) due to expired Google OAuth refresh token which prevents any discovery run from executing sources. The blocker's root cause is a pre-existing credential issue (token expired 2026-04-10) that requires re-authentication with Google, outside subagent scope to resolve. The routing logic is verified as correct; the blocker is the OAuth credential, not the routing implementation.
